question: What's on your product roadmap? What have you promised but not yet delivered?
title: Roadmap Transparency, Delivery Track Record, and Vaporware Risk
bullets: Provide a concrete roadmap with named features, scopes, and realistic timelines (quarters/releases)
Disclose delayed or slipped features with reasons and revised timelines
Show historical delivery performance (on-time %, shipped vs. planned) and evidence (release notes)
Clarify prioritization model (customer input, votes) and explicitly out-of-scope items
suggested_inputs: Public or NDA roadmap with timelines and statuses
Roadmap tracking metrics (on-time delivery %, slip rates)

good.text: Roadmap lists specific features with defined scope and timelines (e.g., Q2/Q3 releases). Items are categorized (committed vs. exploratory). Customers can view a public/NDA roadmap with status updates.
We explicitly disclose delayed features, reasons for slippage, and revised timelines. We avoid selling features not yet shipped and differentiate clearly between GA, beta, and planned items.
We provide historical delivery metrics (e.g., on-time % over last 12 months) with release notes showing shipped vs. planned items, including any misses and corrective actions.
Roadmap prioritization incorporates customer feedback (voting/CSM inputs) and strategic goals. We explicitly state what we will not build and why, to avoid ambiguity.

partial.text: We share a high-level roadmap, but timelines are vague or not tied to specific releases.
We acknowledge some delays, but do not consistently publish reasons, revised timelines, or status updates.
We provide release notes, but lack clear metrics on on-time delivery or planned vs. shipped comparisons.
Customer input is considered, but the prioritization model and decision criteria are not transparent.

bad.text: Multiple features are described as 'coming soon' or 'in the next release' without firm timelines.
We do not maintain or share a product roadmap or timelines.
We present planned features as if they are already available or guaranteed imminently.

follow_ups: Provide your public/NDA roadmap and a 12-month view of shipped vs. planned features.
What is your on-time delivery rate over the last 12 months? Provide evidence.
Which features have been 'in progress' the longest and why?
How do customers influence roadmap priorities (voting, councils, CSM input)?
What features are explicitly out of scope and will not be built?
